body_txt: This trope is when hairdressing and haircare (accessories, arrangements, styling, maintenance, and ornamentation ) are part of cultural, religious, and tribal identity. Styles of hair and facial hair carry a lot of cultural significance and importance for the wearer because it's a vital part of their ancestry, heritage, identity, and traditions. 2 variations of this exist: Doctrine and Tradition. A doctrine implies that it is a requirement for the culture to reflect alignment and commitment. Tradition implies that it is a long-standing custom for communal identity and self-expression.  Ethnicity and race are deeply ingrained in cultural grooming traditions. In the modern day, barbershops and salons act as social hubs, personal sanctuaries, and cultural touchstones. Members of these cultures feel more comfortable having their needs met by those who have the necessary knowledge, experience, and empathy to manage and cut their hair. After all, it's an act of social trust and faithfulness to cut someone's hair. These touchstones help those who feel homesick and help address personal anxieties and stress around identity and cultural disconnection . Because of the significance of these styles in their traditions, the act of fiddling, playing with, and touching someone's hair without their consent is not just rude — it's an invasion of personal space and boundaries, a serious affront to their traditions and beliefs. Altering, forcefully cutting , or touching someone's hair is not just profoundly offensive and upsetting — it's a hate crime because it targets a person's culture, identity, and religion. For some cultures, the demonym may have earned the right to wear such a hairstyle by achieving a rite of passage, while others wear the hairstyle as a cultural identifier to honor their cultural origins. To see an outsider adopt these hairstyles, either accidentally or thoughtlessly, is treated as cultural appropriation by the cultural demonym because they believe outsiders are ignoring the historical context and significance. It's sacrilegious because they believe it poses the risk of defiling and disgracing a sacred practice. Haircare helps address complex issues of heritage and tradition. Dress codes , grooming policies , forced assimilation , and misinformation about hair types have contributed to cultural insensitivity and erasure. Military service is the most prominent in these conversations because the initiation process involves shaving a recruit's head and facial hair for uniformity, it was also originally for cleanliness and hygiene reasons because the barracks were frequently infested with lice. Because these cultures faced historical injustices and cultural trauma, their modern-day descendants wear these hairstyles and facial hair styles as a means of reclamation and retention, to preserve and honor their culture. In regards to races in fantasy stories, gnomes and dwarves stand out in particular because their thick beards are frequently depicted as a topic of pride in gnome and dwarven cultures. These cultures state and show how both men and women can grow beards, with added implications being that they are born with beards . They believe beards should be kept long and luxuriant, with some stories integrating beards into their courtship and marriage practices. When the culture is extraterrestrial, then it goes with Alien Hair and Planet of Hats . For worldbuilding in fantasy stories, this is connected to Fantasy Counterpart Culture . See also Fictional Traditions when this trope applies to fictional groups. Subtrope of Distinctive Appearances and Meaningful Appearance . Compare Hereditary Hairstyle when a particular hairstyle is unique to a family. Can go with Good Hair, Evil Hair , Expository Hairstyle Change , and Earned Stripes . Not to be confused with Group-Identifying Feature , which is when groups and cultures have distinct outfits. Also, not to be confused with People of Hair Color , which is specifically about hair color. It can be related to Manly Facial Hair , if the culture considers the facial hair manly. Contrast Nonconformist Dyed Hair , unless they are part of a specific culture or alignment that requires dying the hair. See also Hairstyle Control for when a person or culture shows their dominance over people by enforcing a particular hairstyle. For gang culture, see Gang of Hats .
